meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't validate ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't validate ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Idea Validation
content.seo_description: Learn how to use SaaS pricing to overcome validation hurdles. A beginner-level guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing if your idea has legs. By implementing tiered SaaS pricing early, you force users to commit, providing immediate market validation. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to automate your deployment, turning passive interest into real revenue signals in under two hours.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance projects is filled with 'perfect' apps that nobody actually wanted. You spend weeks polishing a UI, only to launch into the deafening silence of zero paid users. The issue isn't your code; it's your lack of a market feedback loop.

True validation happens the moment a credit card clears. By integrating professional SaaS pricing tiers into your MVP, you shift from 'building for friends' to 'building for customers.' It’s time to stop treating your projects like hobbies and start treating them like high-velocity validation engines.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Don't overcomplicate. Create three tiers: Free (Lead Gen), Pro (Core Value), and Enterprise (Custom). This structure clarifies exactly what problem you are solving.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for price, features array, and stripe_price_id.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a pricing-tiers.json file based on the provided schema'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Gateway
steps.description: Connect your app to Stripe using Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code for checkout sessions and webhook listeners.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js API route for Stripe checkout sessions that handles user authentication and tier-based pricing redirection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add stripe-sdk --task 'Implement the checkout session logic in /api/checkout'

steps.title: Implement Paywall Logic
steps.description: Gate your premium features. If a user tries to access a restricted route without a valid subscription, they get redirected to the pricing page.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function for Next.js that checks if a user's subscription status in the database matches the required plan level.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor middleware.ts --task 'Add subscription gating logic'

steps.title: Automate Webhook Fulfillment
steps.description: Ensure your database updates automatically when a payment is successful. This is the 'set it and forget it' part of your freelance workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust Stripe webhook handler that updates the 'subscription_status' and 'plan_type' fields in a Supabase database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create the webhook listener endpoint and verify signature'

steps.title: Deploy and Test
steps.description: Push your code to production and perform a live test with Stripe's test mode to ensure the feedback loop is fully operational.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that configures environment variables for Stripe and Supabase in a Vercel environment.

pro_tips: Always price your lowest tier at a 'no-brainer' price point to maximize early user acquisition data.
Use Stripe's 'Customer Portal' instead of building custom subscription management pages to save 10+ hours of UI work.
Never build a custom auth system; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to handle subscription-linked user identities.
Add a 'Contact Us' button to your Enterprise tier to gather high-intent leads even before you have the feature built.
Focus on 'Time-to-First-Dollar' as your primary metric—the faster you get paid, the faster you validate.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, share your project on IndieHackers or Twitter to drive traffic and track conversion rates. Use the data to iterate on your features.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for profit. Our community provides the templates, logic, and peer support you need to ship faster than the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
